meta.title: Niwa Ski Area
meta.description: Free rope tow in Setana, Hokkaido. Weeknight skiing 18:00–21:00, ungroomed snow, and a Showa-era local hill.

hero.title:
Niwa
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Free rope tow—weeknights from 18:00, a tiny hill in the middle of the snowfield.

sanctuary.title:
A hill that appears
at the end of the snowfield.
sanctuary.lead: Walk a snowy path from parking to a ~200 m square hill. Ungroomed natural snow and a working Showa-era rope tow—a world apart from southern Hokkaido's big resorts.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Days and hours change with snow and weather. Check today's dashboard or Setana town notices before you go.
sanctuary.imgAlt: A small hill floating in the snowfield—Niwa Ski Area

primary.title: Completely free rope tow
primary.lead: No lift ticket required. Staff often run the rope tow even for solo visitors—a small municipal hill rooted in the local community.
primary.imgAlt: Riders holding the rope on Niwa's tow

secondary.title: Weeknight skiing 18–21
secondary.lead: Weekdays are lit nights only, 18:00–21:00. A quick after-work or after-school lap on an irregular schedule.

pass.title:
Lift fees have
always been free.
pass.lead: No charge to ski. Donation boxes or sticker programs may support upkeep—check on-site notices when you arrive.
pass.imgAlt: Lodge and rope tow at the free hill

hike.title:
Climb with your arms,
pure rope tow.
hike.lead: Not a chair or T-bar—you grip the rope and pull yourself up the old-fashioned way. Three laps can be enough for sore arms. Snowboards use a separate ~150 m rope course, not the main slope.
hike.leadCurry: After skiing, Kitahiyama Onsen Hotel or Mitsuba Shokudo nearby—food and baths finish the Setana loop.
hike.imgAlt: Showa-era rope tow—the symbol of Niwa Ski Area

tour.title:
Kitahiyama area
in half a day.
tour.lead: Rope tow at Niwa → Kitahiyama Onsen → Setana meal—enjoy the area as a small trip, not just the hill.

01.title: Niwa Ski Area
01.body: Walk the snowy approach from parking

sanctuary.title: Niwa rope tow guide
sanctuary.body: How to use the pure rope, snowboard course limits, and the walk from parking—essentials for first visits.

workation.title: Reading irregular hours
workation.body: Weeknights 18–21, weekends ~10–17—today's dashboard beats digging through PDF town bulletins.

lake.title: Hidden hills of southern Hokkaido
lake.body: How Niwa compares with Taisei Ski Area (sea views) and Pikari in Imakane—why choose Niwa.

access.note: Drive from Kitahiyama center · walk from parking across snow
access.phoneOps: Check today's dashboard or town notices for hours
